# Implementation Plan: Media Library Home Sections

**Branch**: `005-media-library-home` | **Date**: March 30, 2026 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/005-media-library-home/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command following the specification and clarification workflow.

## Summary

Add three new media library sections to the home page: Photos (5-item slider with popup viewer and navigation), Videos (6 YouTube videos with custom thumbnails and popup player), and Partners (configurable slider with clickable cards). Each section includes full admin management with bilingual support (English/Arabic), visibility controls, featured flags, publish dates, and audit trails. Gallery pages display all items with most-recent-first sorting. Follows existing project patterns for News and SliderContent sections.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript with Next.js 16.1.6, React 19.2.3  
**Primary Dependencies**: Next.js App Router, Prisma ORM 7.5.0, @clerk/nextjs 7.0.4, @vercel/blob 2.3.1, TailwindCSS 4  
**Storage**: Neon PostgreSQL via Prisma with @neondatabase/serverless, Vercel Blob for media files  
**Testing**: Vitest with @testing-library/react, jsdom  
**Target Platform**: Web (responsive - mobile, tablet, desktop)  
**Project Type**: Full-stack web application (SSR + API routes)  
**Performance Goals**: <1s initial photo load, <500ms popup open, <100ms slider transitions  
**Constraints**: 5MB photo limit, 5MB video thumbnail limit, bilingual support (EN/AR), RTL layout for Arabic  
**Scale/Scope**: 3 new database tables (photos, videos, partners), 6 API routes, 9 React components, 4 pages

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|-----------|-------------|--------|----------|
| **I. Client & Order Management First** | Features prioritize usability and data integrity. Operations must be auditable, reversible (soft deletes), maintain referential integrity | ✅ **PASS** | Photos/videos/partners use soft delete (is_deleted flag), audit trails (created_by, updated_by, created_at, updated_at), no foreign keys to preserve data integrity |
| **II. Clerk Authentication & Security** | User authentication via Clerk mandatory. All API routes verify Clerk session tokens. User identity captured in timestamps | ✅ **PASS** | All API routes will use Clerk auth middleware, created_by/updated_by fields capture admin identity |
| **III. Neon PostgreSQL as Source of Truth** | Schema is contractual source of truth. Changes use zero-downtime migrations with backward compatibility | ✅ **PASS** | New tables (photos, videos, partners) follow existing schema patterns. Migrations add new tables without modifying existing ones |
| **IV. Full-Featured Pages & Search** | Collections require dashboard metrics, list page with search, add/edit/view/delete pages, export capability | ✅ **PASS** | Photos/videos/partners each get admin CRUD pages, integrated into dashboard metrics, searchable in gallery pages. Export capability follows existing ExcelJS pattern from clients |
| **V. Order History & Client Relationship** | Order history mandatory for clients | ✅ **N/A** | Not applicable - this feature manages media content, not client/order data |
| **VI. API-First Development** | Frontend consumes documented REST/JSON APIs. Business logic in API routes with validation | ✅ **PASS** | All CRUD operations via API routes: /api/photos, /api/videos, /api/partners, /api/home-sections, input validation, proper HTTP status codes |
| **VII. Dashboard & Analytics** | Dashboard as default landing page with real-time metrics | ✅ **PASS** | Dashboard will display total counts for photos, videos, partners (following existing newsCount pattern) |

**Overall**: ✅ **ALL GATES PASS** - No violations. Feature aligns with all applicable constitution principles.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/005-media-library-home/
├── spec.md              # Feature specification (completed)
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (technology decisions)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (database schema)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (implementation guide)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (API contracts)
│   ├── api-photos.md
│   ├── api-videos.md
│   ├── api-partners.md
│   └── api-home-sections.md
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks - NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
# Next.js App Router structure (existing pattern)
app/
├── page.tsx                    # Home page (add Photos, Videos, Partners sections)
├── api/
│   ├── photos/
│   │   └── route.ts           # GET, POST /api/photos
│   ├── videos/
│   │   └── route.ts           # GET, POST /api/videos
│   ├── partners/
│   │   └── route.ts           # GET, POST /api/partners
│   └── home-sections/
│       └── route.ts           # GET, PUT /api/home-sections (visibility config)
├── admin/
│   ├── layout.tsx             # Add photos/videos/partners tabs
│   ├── photos/
│   │   └── page.tsx           # Photos admin CRUD interface
│   ├── videos/
│   │   └── page.tsx           # Videos admin CRUD interface
│   └── partners/
│       └── page.tsx           # Partners admin CRUD interface
├── photos/
│   └── page.tsx               # Public photo gallery page
├── videos/
│   └── page.tsx               # Public video gallery page
└── partners/
    └── page.tsx               # Public partners directory page

lib/
├── components/
│   ├── home/
│   │   ├── PhotosSection.tsx  # 5-photo slider on home page
│   │   ├── VideosSection.tsx  # 6-video grid on home page
│   │   └── PartnersSection.tsx # Partners slider on home page
│   ├── media/
│   │   ├── PhotoPopup.tsx     # Full-screen photo viewer with navigation
│   │   └── VideoPopup.tsx     # YouTube player popup with navigation
│   └── admin/
│       ├── PhotoForm.tsx      # Photo add/edit form
│       ├── VideoForm.tsx      # Video add/edit form
│       └── PartnerForm.tsx    # Partner add/edit form
└── types.ts                   # Add Photo, Video, Partner, HomeSection types

prisma/
├── schema.prisma              # Add photos, videos, partners, home_sections tables
└── migrations/
    └── YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_add_media_library_tables/
        └── migration.sql

public/
└── placeholders/
    └── video-thumbnail-default.png  # Default video thumbnail placeholder
```

**Structure Decision**: Following existing Next.js App Router pattern with API routes in `app/api/`, admin pages in `app/admin/`, public pages at app root, and reusable components in `lib/components`. Media storage via Vercel Blob (existing infrastructure), database tables via Prisma migrations (existing ORM setup).
